evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of FGFR1-related Hartsfield syndrome is established in a proband with suggestive findings and either an FGFR1 heterozygous pathogenic variant (in those with autosomal dominant inheritance) or FGFR1 biallelic pathogenic variants (in those with autosomal recessive inheritance) identified by molecular genetic testing.
FGFR1 | HGNC:3688 | Hartsfield-Bixler-Demyer syndrome | MONDO:0014196 | AD | Moderate

nodes.description: Pathogenic variants in FGFR1 disrupt signaling in the anterior neural ridge during early embryogenesis, impairing forebrain midline patterning. This leads to failed or incomplete division of the prosencephalon (holoprosencephaly), ranging from alobar to lobar forms, as well as corpus callosum agenesis and absent olfactory bulbs.

evidence.snippet: FGFR1-related Hartsfield syndrome comprises two core features: holoprosencephaly (HPE) spectrum disorder and ectrodactyly spectrum disorder.

nodes.description: The same FGFR1 pathogenic variants that disrupt forebrain development also impair signaling in the apical ectodermal ridge during limb bud patterning. This leads to ectrodactyly (split-hand/foot malformation) due to defective central ray formation.

evidence.snippet: FGFR1-related Hartsfield syndrome comprises two core features: holoprosencephaly (HPE) spectrum disorder and ectrodactyly spectrum disorder.
